senses.definition: to suggest something for discussion or consideration

examples.x: The employee brought forward a cost-saving suggestion to the management.
John brought forward several ideas for improving workplace productivity.
The CEO brought forward a plan to boost company morale.

senses.definition: to save something for later use or consideration

examples.x: I carried forward the skills I learned in my previous job to excel in my new role.
She carried forward the savings from last month to cover unexpected expenses.
The lessons learned from failures should be carried forward for continuous improvement.
The company policy allows employees to carry forward unused vacation days to the next year.

senses.definition: to advance to the next stage of a competition or process, especially by winning a preliminary round or meeting certain requirements

examples.x: The team's victory in the semifinals allowed them to go forward to the championship match.
Only those who pass the initial screening interviews will go forward in the job application process.
Her excellent performance in the auditions earned her a chance to go forward to the final round of the talent competition.
The top three candidates will go forward to the next round of the debate tournament.
Winning the regional qualifiers means they can go forward to represent their school at the national competition.

senses.definition: to make progress or advance in a positive direction

examples.x: After resolving the initial challenges, the team is ready to move forward with the project.
Last quarter, the company successfully moved forward with its expansion plans.
She frequently thinks about how to move forward in her career.
Teams often discuss how to move forward after completing a phase of the project.
He has been contemplating how to move forward in his personal development.

senses.definition: to present an idea, suggestion, etc. to be discussed

examples.x: She put forward a new plan to increase sales.
He put forward a motion to adjourn the meeting.
Why don't you put forward your idea at the next team meeting?
The committee put forward new guidelines for remote work.

senses.definition: to give birth and bring into being

examples.x: The animal brought forth several adorable puppies.
The cat proudly brought forth a litter of kittens.
The young couple eagerly anticipated the day they would bring forth their first child.
After a long labor, she joyfully brought forth a healthy baby girl.
The midwife assisted the mother as she brought forth her son in the birthing center.

senses.definition: to appear, emerge, or be revealed

examples.x: The magician waved his wand, and a dove came forth from his hat.
As the investigation progressed, new evidence began to come forth, shedding light on the case.
The flowers bloomed, and their vibrant colors came forth, adding beauty to the garden.

senses.definition: to release or produce something, like smoke, sounds, or aromas

examples.x: The volcano gave forth molten lava during the eruption.
The flowers in the garden gave forth a fragrant aroma in the spring.
The chemical reaction in the lab equipment gave forth a noxious gas.
The violin gave forth a beautiful melody.

senses.definition: to talk at length about a topic, often in a manner that others might find uninteresting or boring

examples.x: At the family gathering, Uncle Bob held forth about his extensive stamp collection, leaving everyone looking for an escape.
The professor held forth on the intricacies of quantum physics, losing most of the class within minutes.
As the dinner party host, Sarah held forth on her recent travels, sharing endless stories that fascinated her but seemed never-ending to her guests.
During the lecture, the guest speaker held forth about the history of ancient civilizations, captivating the audience with his expertise.
Whenever Bill gets started on his conspiracy theories, he can hold forth for hours, oblivious to the eye rolls from his friends.

senses.definition: to present information or arguments in a coherent and clear manner

examples.x: Set forth your ideas in the essay, providing evidence to support your claims.
The CEO set forth the company's vision for the future in a compelling presentation.
The lawyer set forth the case's key points in a persuasive manner during the trial.
